message:
test(fcp-core): pin RevocationCheckResult + RevocationScope serde (flywheel_connectors-8gfcv)

Bead asks for RegistryQuery serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip. fcp-core has
no type literally named RegistryQuery; the closest "query/lookup
result" type with serde derives is RevocationCheckResult
(revocation.rs:409) — the result of looking up an ObjectId against
the revocation registry. Carries 5 fields (3 scalar + 2 Option<>
with skip_serializing_if) and a nested RevocationScope enum.
RegistryEntry (the other registry-shaped type) is already pinned
by connector_bundle_serde_extended.rs (huv65); this test focuses
on the lookup-result surface that's still a gap.

Coverage:
- RevocationCheckResult JSON shape pinned for both revoked
  (Optional fields populated) and not-revoked (Optional fields
  omitted via skip_serializing_if) cases
- JSON round-trip preserves every field including stale_data flag
  combined with revoked-by-stale-registry fixture (head_age_secs
  at u64::MAX boundary)
- CBOR round-trip preserves every field for both cases
- RevocationScope per-variant snake_case Display token pinned
  (capability/issuer_key/node_attestation/zone_key/connector_binary)
- RevocationScope serde JSON form is PascalCase variant name
  verbatim — DIFFERENT from Display/as_str (snake_case). Same
  dual-encoding pattern as RiskTier; pinned loud as drift sentinel
- Display vs serde disagreement on multi-word variants pinned
  explicitly (issuer_key vs IssuerKey)
- RevocationScope JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant; CBOR encodes
  as Text PascalCase
- Lower snake_case rejected for all multi-word variants (would
  alias as_str output but is NOT the wire form)
- Distinct check results produce distinct JSON (scope, head_age_secs
  axes both observable on the wire)
- 5-variant count sentinel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
